skill.name: mcp-builder
skill.description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
skill.summary: Build well-designed MCP servers for external APIs using TypeScript or Python guidance.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official source review found documentation examples for network calls, environment variables, and external commands. These are expected for MCP server development guidance and evaluation scripts. No prompt injection attempts or malicious behavior were found.

content.value_statement: MCP server builders need clear patterns for tool design, API coverage, testing, and evaluations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical server planning and implementation.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to plan MCP tools around real API workflows.
Provides TypeScript and Python implementation guidance through reference files.
Covers tool schemas, annotations, pagination, error handling, and response formatting.
Guides setup for streamable HTTP and stdio MCP transports.
Describes how to create read-only evaluation questions and expected answers.
Includes helper scripts for connecting to MCP servers and running evaluations.
limitations: Does not generate a complete production server without service-specific API details.
Requires current MCP SDK documentation for the newest protocol behavior.
Evaluation examples need adaptation to each target service and dataset.
Security, authentication, and deployment choices still require project review.

use_cases.title: Plan a New API Connector
use_cases.description: Map an external service API into useful MCP tools with clear names, schemas, and coverage priorities.

use_cases.title: Implement a Typed MCP Server
use_cases.description: Use TypeScript or Python patterns to create tools, shared clients, validation models, and structured responses.

use_cases.title: Create MCP Quality Evaluations
use_cases.description: Design realistic read-only questions that test whether agents can use the server to answer complex tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Server Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new MCP server.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan an MCP server for this API. Identify the core resources, the first tools to build, and any authentication concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Design Tool Schemas
prompt_templates.prompt: Design MCP tool names, input schemas, output shapes, and annotations for these API endpoints. Focus on clear agent usability.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing server.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this MCP server implementation for naming, pagination, error handling, schema quality, transport choice, and missing API coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Build Evaluation Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 10 independent read-only evaluation questions for this MCP server. Include expected answers and explain how each answer can be verified.

output_examples.input: Plan an MCP server for a ticketing API.
output: A prioritized tool list for projects, tickets, comments, users, and search.
Recommended read-only and write tools with clear destructive annotations.
Notes on pagination, authentication, and useful error messages.
Specific description improvements for discoverability and parameter clarity.
Warnings about vague names, missing constraints, and oversized responses.
Suggestions for structured output and consistent naming prefixes.

output_examples.input: Create evaluation questions for the completed server.
output: Ten realistic read-only questions that require multiple tool calls.
A verified answer for each question.
Guidance for keeping answers stable over time.

best_practices: Start with API research and map tools to real user workflows.
Use strict schemas, actionable errors, and pagination for large responses.
Test with MCP Inspector and create read-only evaluations before release.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw endpoint wrappers without clear agent-facing descriptions.
Do not return unlimited data when filters or pagination are available.
Do not mark destructive tools as safe or read-only.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps build MCP servers that connect AI tools to external APIs and services.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript and Python?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for TypeScript MCP SDK patterns and Python FastMCP patterns.

faq.question: Can it choose the exact tools for my API?
faq.answer: It can help choose tools after you provide API documentation, endpoint details, and workflow goals.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers build checks, MCP Inspector testing, and evaluation question design.

faq.question: Is this only for local MCP servers?
faq.answer: No. It discusses stdio for local servers and streamable HTTP for remote servers.

faq.question: Does it handle production security automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives design guidance, but authentication, authorization, and deployment security need separate review.
